user_query:
I'm a solo marketing consultant trying to scale by automating my weekly routine. Please read the following files and produce a complete automation plan with concrete blueprints and ROI:

- Read input/tasks.csv (one-week task log) and input/tool_constraints.yaml (budget, preferences, hourly_rate), plus input/current_stack.json (my current tools).

Deliverables to write under output/:
1) output/automation_audit.json — Build an automation audit from input/tasks.csv. For each task, include: task_name, minutes_per_task, frequency_per_month (convert from weekly), time_cost_hours = (minutes_per_task × frequency_per_month)/60, repetitive (true/false), requires_judgment (true/false), automation_candidate (true if repetitive and not judgment-heavy and done > 2 times/week). Sort tasks by time_cost_hours descending.
2) output/workflows.json — Propose at least 3 high-impact automation workflows. For each, include the following fields exactly:
   - name (string)
   - trigger (string)
   - conditions (string or array)
   - actions (array of 2–5 actionable steps)
   - error_handling (clear notification/alert plan)
   - platform_category (one of: "simple-2-3-steps", "visual-multi-step", "self-hosted-advanced")
   - test_plan (how you will test each step and edge cases)
   - monitoring (how to track runs and failures)
   - roi: { setup_hours, time_saved_hours_per_month, tool_monthly_cost, payback_months }
   Compute payback_months as setup_hours / time_saved_hours_per_month (ignore subscription cost in the payback calculation). Use the hourly_rate from input/tool_constraints.yaml for value commentary in the write-up, but the payback formula should be exactly the ratio above.
   Ensure at least one workflow is a simple MVP (2–3 actions) and all workflows include explicit error notifications.
3) output/monitoring_plan.md — A concise monitoring and maintenance plan that includes: weekly checks of run/error logs, monthly workflow audits with a checklist, and where documentation will live. Add specific actions tied to the proposed workflows.
4) output/checklist.txt — A short, bullet list of best practices to keep in front of me. Include these exact lines as separate bullets:
   - Start with simple 2-3 step workflows
   - Always include error notifications
   - Test with edge cases
   - Don't automate before optimizing

Use the audit to justify which workflows you prioritize and choose a platform_category that fits my constraints in input/tool_constraints.yaml. Make sure your choices align with my budget and preferences. Keep everything vendor-agnostic (do not name specific products).
